rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when referring to component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers from the input AADL model for AGREE annex blocks
generic_rules: Preserve the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Ensure that all identifiers used in AGREE annex blocks are visible in the target scope
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type contracts when concrete bounds are provided
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Avoid encoding AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
Do not encode AADL property names as AGREE variables without explicit exposure
